key_drivers: The dollar remains a broad cross-asset tailwind: the accessible Yahoo Finance chart feed showed DXY at 98.10, down 0.12% on the day and 0.56% over five sessions.
Risk conditions are constructive rather than euphoric because VIX fell to 17.48, MOVE eased to 65.70, and SOX climbed 2.43% on the day and 7.49% over five sessions.

Crude keeps a firm macro backdrop because the latest official EIA weekly highlights showed a 0.9 million barrel draw in U.S. commercial crude inventories and a broader 9.0 million barrel drop in total commercial petroleum inventories.

FX leadership remains supported by policy divergence: the Fed is still best classified as a neutral hold, while the ECB held a firm anti-inflation line, the RBA hiked to 4.10%, and the BoJ remains in a normalization regime at 0.75%.

stale_sources: Atlanta Fed GDPNow is still the April 9, 2026 official update at 1.3% for 2026:Q1.
FRED DFII10 and BAMLH0A0HYM2 latest official observations are dated April 16, 2026, reflecting normal publication lag.
World Gold Council ETF-flow evidence is still the March 2026 monthly release.
CME FedWatch did not provide a directly readable probability table in-session, so the Fed stance used a conservative neutral-hold interpretation consistent with same-week repository evidence.
fallbacks_used: Yahoo Finance chart endpoints were used to refresh DXY, VIX, MOVE, SOX, and copper direction in a single accessible format.
The People's Daily summary of official NBS data was used to confirm the March rebound in China manufacturing PMI to 50.4.
Eurozone growth was kept at a conservative stable classification because no fully readable official same-session PMI release was obtained during this run.
